status_notes: Initial research from AWS EC2 documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference pass and human review with live AWS environment.
notes: Virtual machines. Security includes instance metadata service, security groups, key pairs, EBS encryption, Systems Manager, Nitro Enclaves.

match_nodes.description: Instance Metadata Service v2 — session-oriented, token-based metadata access

data.description: Requires session tokens (PUT request before GET) for instance metadata access. Mitigates SSRF attacks that could steal credentials from the metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254).

options.name: IMDSv2 required
options.description: Only token-based (PUT+GET) requests accepted. Blocks IMDSv1 GET-only requests.

options.name: IMDSv2 optional
options.description: Both IMDSv1 (GET-only) and IMDSv2 (PUT+GET) accepted. Legacy default.

default.note: Required for new instance types (2024+), optional for older. AWS is migrating all instances to IMDSv2-only.

data.vendor_recommendation: Require IMDSv2, disable IMDSv1. Set http-tokens to required and http-put-response-hop-limit to 1 (prevents container credential theft via network hops).

audit.console: EC2 → Instances → {instance} → Actions → Instance settings → Modify instance metadata options

match_nodes.description: Instance-level stateful firewall controlling inbound and outbound traffic

data.description: Virtual firewalls for EC2 instances. Stateful — return traffic is automatically allowed. Rules specify protocol, port range, and source/destination (CIDR, security group ID, or prefix list). Default: deny all inbound, allow all outbound.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use least-privilege rules. Avoid 0.0.0.0/0 for inbound SSH/RDP. Use security group references instead of CIDR where possible for service-to-service communication.

match_nodes.description: SSH key pairs for Linux instance access

data.description: Public/private key pairs for SSH access to Linux instances. Public key stored on instance, private key held by user. Options: RSA (2048/4096-bit) or ED25519.

options.name: ED25519
options.description: Smaller, faster. Not supported on all AMIs.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Systems Manager Session Manager instead of SSH keys where possible. If SSH keys are required, use ED25519, rotate regularly, and never share private keys.

data.description: Encrypt data at rest on EBS volumes attached to EC2 instances. Uses AES-256. Encryption happens on the server hosting the instance (data encrypted in transit between instance and volume).

options.name: AWS managed key
options.description: Default aws/ebs KMS key. No additional cost.

options.name: Customer managed key
options.description: Your KMS key. Allows key policy control and rotation.

default.note: Not encrypted by default unless account-level default encryption is enabled.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable account-level default EBS encryption. Use customer-managed keys for sensitive workloads.

match_nodes.description: IAM roles attached to EC2 instances providing temporary credentials

data.description: Container for an IAM role that passes role information to an EC2 instance at launch. The instance can then use the role's temporary credentials (via IMDS) to call AWS APIs without embedding access keys.
data.vendor_recommendation: Never embed access keys in instances — use instance profiles. One role per instance with least-privilege permissions. Use IAM Access Analyzer to right-size permissions.

match_nodes.description: Patch management, remote access without SSH, compliance scanning

data.description: AWS Systems Manager provides operational management for EC2 instances. Session Manager provides audited shell access without open inbound ports or SSH keys. Patch Manager automates OS and application patching. Compliance reports on patch and configuration status.

options.description: Interactive shell, no SSH, no open ports. All sessions logged to CloudTrail/S3/CloudWatch.
Automated patching with patch baselines, maintenance windows, and compliance reporting.
Enforce desired state configurations on instances.
Collect metadata about instances: applications, network, OS, services.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Session Manager instead of SSH/RDP for all administrative access. Enable patch compliance scanning. Require SSM Agent on all instances.

match_nodes.description: Isolated compute environments for highly sensitive data processing

data.description: Isolated virtual machines within an EC2 instance with their own kernel, CPU, and memory. No persistent storage, no interactive access, no external networking. Uses cryptographic attestation. Designed for processing highly sensitive data (PII, financial, healthcare).
data.vendor_recommendation: Use Nitro Enclaves for workloads processing highly sensitive data that require isolation from the parent instance operator.

configure.note: Must be enabled at launch. Requires Nitro-based instance types.

match_nodes.description: Troubleshooting access via serial port — disabled by default

data.description: Interactive serial console access for troubleshooting boot issues, network misconfigurations, and SSH problems. Text-based, low-bandwidth. Disabled by default at the account level.

default.note: Disabled by default at account level. Must be explicitly enabled.

data.vendor_recommendation: Keep disabled unless actively troubleshooting. Enable temporarily, then disable when done.

match_nodes.description: Control who can launch instances from your AMIs

data.description: Amazon Machine Image sharing controls. AMIs can be private (owner only, default), shared with specific accounts, or public. Public AMIs are visible to all AWS accounts.

options.name: Private
options.description: Only the owning account can launch. Default.

options.name: Public
options.description: Any AWS account can launch. Security risk if AMI contains sensitive data.

data.vendor_recommendation: Keep AMIs private unless sharing is required. Never make AMIs public if they contain credentials, keys, or sensitive configurations. Use AWS RAM for controlled sharing across organizations.

match_nodes.description: Control instance placement for isolation or performance

data.description: Influence how instances are placed on underlying hardware. Spread placement groups distribute instances across distinct hardware to reduce correlated failures. Partition groups separate instances into logical partitions on separate racks.

options.name: Cluster
options.description: Pack instances close together for low-latency networking. Single AZ.

options.name: Spread
options.description: Each instance on distinct hardware. Max 7 per AZ. Reduces correlated failure.

options.name: Partition
options.description: Instances in logical partitions on separate racks. For large distributed workloads (HDFS, HBase, Cassandra).

match_nodes.description: Encryption of hibernated instance RAM contents

data.description: When an EC2 instance hibernates, the contents of RAM are saved to the root EBS volume. This data is encrypted using the EBS volume's encryption key. The root volume must be encrypted for hibernation to be enabled.
data.vendor_recommendation: Ensure root EBS volumes are encrypted before enabling hibernation. Memory contents may contain sensitive data (credentials, keys, application state).

match_nodes.description: Instance initialization scripts — security concern for embedded secrets

data.description: Scripts or cloud-init directives executed at instance launch. Accessible via instance metadata (IMDS). Common security concern: developers embed secrets, API keys, or passwords in user data.
data.vendor_recommendation: Never put secrets in user data. Use AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store for secrets. User data is accessible to anyone who can access the instance metadata.

audit.note: User data is base64-encoded in the response. Decode to inspect for secrets.
